description:
You are an IT-support technician who has just received a ticket complaining that three
text-data files delivered by a vendor are still in legacy Latin-1 (ISO-8859-1) encoding.
Your job is to:

1. Verify the current character encoding of every “*.txt” file inside the directory  
   /home/user/support/incoming/

2. Convert each of those files to UTF-8 **without altering their textual content** and
   place the converted copies in  
   /home/user/support/resolved/  
   (the original files must remain untouched in …/incoming/).

3. Keep the original filenames when writing the converted copies.

4. Create a plain-text, tab-separated log file named  
   /home/user/support/encoding_report.log  
   containing one line per processed file, **with no header line**.
   Each line must have four tab-separated columns in this exact order:

   a) FILE_NAME  (the basename, e.g. “clients_es.txt”)  
   b) ORIGINAL_ENCODING (exactly the string “ISO-8859-1”)  
   c) NEW_ENCODING  (exactly the string “UTF-8”)  
   d) STATUS     (exactly the string “SUCCESS” once the conversion finished)

   Example of one correct line in the finished log (TABs shown as “→” here for clarity):

   clients_es.txt→ISO-8859-1→UTF-8→SUCCESS

5. Ensure that /home/user/support/encoding_report.log ends with a single trailing
   newline (UNIX LF).

Hints & constraints (do NOT print these in the log):
• Only the three files that already exist in “…/incoming/” must be processed.  
• The destination directory “…/resolved/” does not exist yet and must be created.  
• While any tools may be used, prefer standard Linux utilities already present in most
  distributions (e.g. file, iconv, md5sum, etc.).  
• The entire job can be finished with roughly 5-10 well-chosen shell commands.  
• You do not have root privileges; stay inside /home/user.  
• The automated test will validate:  
  – presence of “clients_es.txt”, “résumé_fr.txt” and “daten_de.txt” in
    /home/user/support/resolved/;  
  – that every file in “…/resolved/” is valid UTF-8 and byte-for-byte identical to the
    original text when decoded;  
  – that /home/user/support/encoding_report.log exists, has exactly three lines,
    each with the four specified fields separated by a single TAB, ending with LF,
    and every STATUS field equals “SUCCESS”.

When you are done, all three UTF-8 files must be in “…/resolved/” and the log file
must match the required format exactly.
truth:
Pre-existing directory structure and files before the agent starts:

1. Directory: /home/user/support/incoming/

   a) /home/user/support/incoming/clients_es.txt  
      (ISO-8859-1, 4 lines, content when decoded):

      ID,Nombre,País
      1,Ana,Costa Rica
      2,José,México
      3,María,España

   b) /home/user/support/incoming/résumé_fr.txt  
      (ISO-8859-1, 4 lines, content when decoded):

      ID;Nom;Poste
      1;Zoé;Développeuse
      2;André;Analyste
      3;Hélène;Gestionnaire

   c) /home/user/support/incoming/daten_de.txt  
      (ISO-8859-1, 4 lines, content when decoded):

      ArtikelID|Name|Beschreibung
      1001|Käse|Frischer Gouda
      1002|Würstchen|Brühwurst aus Bayern
      1003|Apfel|Süßer Elstar

No other files or directories exist under /home/user/support/ at start.

Expected final state **after successful task completion**:

1. A new directory: /home/user/support/resolved/

   • /home/user/support/resolved/clients_es.txt  
   • /home/user/support/resolved/résumé_fr.txt  
   • /home/user/support/resolved/daten_de.txt  

   All three files are valid UTF-8, contain the exact same character data as their
   Latin-1 originals, and each ends with a single LF.

2. Log file: /home/user/support/encoding_report.log  
   Exact content (TAB characters between fields, LF at end):

   clients_es.txt<TAB>ISO-8859-1<TAB>UTF-8<TAB>SUCCESS<LF>
   résumé_fr.txt<TAB>ISO-8859-1<TAB>UTF-8<TAB>SUCCESS<LF>
   daten_de.txt<TAB>ISO-8859-1<TAB>UTF-8<TAB>SUCCESS<LF>

No extra blank lines, spaces, or BOM are permitted in the log file.
